scope: The 19 slice records that remain runnable on main after CA-09 closure.
authority: Non-authoritative audit evidence. This file does not change slice status, attest a failed or cancelled run as completed, or replace daemon-owned closure reports.
authorized_by: repository operator in the 2026-07-11 Pi reconciliation session

policy.closed_meaning: accepted historical work published with close metadata and committed evidence
policy.action: Keep every audited slice open until a real acceptance run or an explicitly designed historical-attestation/provenance-repair mechanism closes it. Do not blindly rerun implemented work.

records.rationale: The implementation and ordinary-test confinement guard landed, but the parent run failed and never published closure.
records.revisit_condition: Use a historical-attestation/provenance-repair policy or a bounded acceptance-only run; do not reimplement.

records.rationale: The shared read model landed and CA-06 later strengthened it to a coherent transactional snapshot; the failed parent run did not publish closure.
records.revisit_condition: Use provenance repair or a bounded acceptance-only run.

records.rationale: Layer atomicity intentionally discarded the original ready sibling. Later CA work delivered the attention and transaction-ordering intent more strongly.
records.revisit_condition: Close only if a future slice-retirement state or historical-attestation policy can preserve supersession provenance.

records.rationale: The original worker failed validation, scope, and clippy checks. CA-06 and CA-08 later established projection ownership and typed event compatibility.
records.revisit_condition: Close only through an explicit supersession-aware lifecycle or historical-attestation policy.

records.rationale: Evidence and path-locality work exist, but the required publication/attention/cockpit/replan test-domain split and a current post-CA hotspot measurement are not demonstrated.
records.revisit_condition: Create a bounded validation/closure slice that first remeasures current hotspots and applies only still-valuable YAGNI cleanup.

records.rationale: Pi-first doctrine is present, but the prior roadmap audit explicitly declined to convert source presence into acceptance.
records.revisit_condition: Use a documentation-only acceptance run or an operator-approved historical-attestation policy.

records.rationale: Classifier and one-attempt installed-binary evidence exist, but no accepting slice publication closed the record.
records.revisit_condition: Run bounded acceptance verification or use historical attestation; do not reimplement.

records.rationale: The publication-tip regression landed and later PUB-01B closed, while PUB-01A close metadata/reports were not preserved on main.
records.revisit_condition: Repair closure provenance from immutable run/commit evidence or run acceptance-only validation.

records.rationale: Bounded recovery, authority text, and regressions exist, but no per-slice accepting run or publication trail was found.
records.revisit_condition: Re-run the declared four-command verification in a bounded validation/closure slice.

records.rationale: The daemon run completed and a historical closed record exists, but promotion dropped close metadata from main and the legacy report predates current exit-state requirements.
records.revisit_condition: Use provenance repair that preserves the original run and explicitly records legacy report limitations.

records.rationale: A cancelled terminal run is the required proof outcome; pretending it completed would erase the evidence being tested.
records.revisit_condition: Adopt a human-attested negative-proof exemption protocol before closing.

records.rationale: The historical run completed and proved artifact submission, but its narrow note/merge provenance is absent from main and later default promotion superseded the premise.
records.revisit_condition: Repair provenance without recreating redundant implementation or presenting the obsolete experimental premise as current.

records.rationale: Packaging policy and recorded release checks exist, but no accepting run published this slice.
records.revisit_condition: Use a bounded package-check acceptance run or historical attestation.

records.rationale: The artifact-channel proof landed, but its wrapper-remains-production premise was intentionally superseded by native-TUI default promotion.
records.revisit_condition: Close only through a supersession-aware lifecycle or historical-attestation policy.

records.rationale: The opt-in lifecycle was implemented, then deliberately promoted beyond the experimental/default-wrapper contract.
records.revisit_condition: Close only through a supersession-aware lifecycle or historical-attestation policy.

records.rationale: A failed timeout terminal state is the required proof outcome and must not be rewritten as a completed run.
records.revisit_condition: Adopt a human-attested negative-proof exemption protocol before closing.

records.rationale: Feed projection landed before the parent run was superseded; later coherent projection and shared fixtures revalidated it, but the blocked run cannot supply completion provenance.
records.revisit_condition: Use provenance repair or an acceptance-only run; do not rerun implementation.

records.rationale: Typed painter code exists, but the original run blocked and no clean acceptance record covers every painter fixture, redaction, coalescing, unknown-event, and non-fatal failure criterion.
records.revisit_condition: Run the declared verification and manually attest the Herdr-only non-fatal painter behavior in a bounded validation slice.

records.rationale: Gate projection exists and later read-model work strengthened it, but the blocked parent run never accepted the full active/idle/repair/no-gate fixture and renderer-failure contract.
records.revisit_condition: Run declared verification and attest Herdr portability/failure behavior in a bounded validation slice.

summary.note: TUI-DOGFOOD-01 is counted as provenance repair; REPAIR-01 and painter slices are validation, not fresh implementation. Counts are disposition labels, not workflow completion claims.
